items.title: Security: Add .claudeignore to prevent sensitive files from being transmitted in context

items.title: [security] Built-in secret redaction at jsonl write-time in ~/.claude/projects/
items.desc:
## Summary

Claude Code persists every sub-agent transcript to `~/.claude/projects/<id>/*.jsonl` and records tool stdout **verbatim**. This becomes a leak vector when:

items.title: Content filter blocks legitimate defensive security tool development
items.desc:
## Summary

Claude Opus 4.7 (`claude-opus-4-7`, 1M context) returns `API Error: Output blocked by content filtering policy` repeatedly while implementing a defensive security tool whose entire purpose is preventing harmful cryptocurrency transactions. The classifier appears to read the threat-model vocabulary common to all crypto security tools as adversarial, even though every named scenario in the loaded context exists so the system can refuse, detect, or surface it to the user.

## Reproducti...
items.llm_title: Claude Code 阻碍防御性安全工具开发
items.llm_summary: Claude Opus 4.7 的内容过滤策略过于严格，将开发用于防御恶意加密货币交易的安全工具的正当代码和上下文，误判为有害内容并阻止输出。这实质上阻断了安全研究人员利用该 AI 工具进行合法防御性安全工作。
items.llm_reproduction:
1. 在 Claude Code 中，加载一个涉及加密货币安全工具开发的项目上下文，其中包含威胁模型词汇。
2. 尝试让 AI 实现该工具的防御性功能（如检测、拒绝有害交易）。
3. AI 反复返回“API Error: Output blocked by content filtering policy”，导致开发流程中断。

items.title: feat(security/exec): add tools.exec.denyPathPatterns hard-deny gate (#74379)
items.desc:
## Summary

Adds an opt-in `tools.exec.denyPathPatterns` — a config-level deny gate that blocks `exec` / `system.run` invocations whose path arguments match configured glob patterns, **before** allowlist/approval/safe-bin checks. A denied path (e.g. `~/.openclaw/secrets/**`, `**/.ssh/id_*`) cannot be reached via `security="full"`, allow-always, or safe-bins.

items.title: [Bug]: `exec` tool schema exposes `security`/`elevated`/`ask` fields as model-controllable; model self-imposes denial
items.desc:
## Summary

The `exec` tool schema in OpenClaw 2026.4.9 exposes the `security` field (enum: `deny` / `allowlist` / `default`, possibly others) as part of the tool's argument surface visible to the model. Smaller models — in our testing, Hermes-3-Llama-3.1-8B served via vLLM — populate this field non-deterministically when generating an `exec` toolCall, sampling from the enum's values. The gateway honors the model-supplied value, returns a denial when the value is restrictive, and the model then ...

items.llm_reproduction: 1. 使用如Hermes-3-Llama-3.1-8B等较小模型。 2. 触发模型生成调用`exec`工具的工具调用。 3. 观察模型是否会在工具参数中随机或尝试填充`security`字段（如设为`deny`）。 4. 网关（如OpenClaw）若采纳了模型提供的该字段值，则会导致命令执行被拒绝。

items.title: [Security] Ship a dedicated/bundled Node binary so macOS TCC permissions (FDA, Accessibility) are scoped to OpenClaw only
items.desc:
## Problem

When users grant **Full Disk Access** (or Accessibility) to OpenClaw on macOS, they must grant it to the shared `node` binary (e.g. `/usr/local/bin/node` or an nvm-managed path). macOS TCC has no mechanism to scope that permission to OpenClaw specifically — the grant applies to **every process that runs through the same binary**, including:

- All globally installed npm packages
- All `node_modules` in any project on the machine
- Any supply-chain-compromised dependency

This means a...
items.llm_title: OpenClaw macOS TCC 权限范围泄露
items.llm_summary: 在macOS上授予OpenClaw全盘访问或辅助功能权限时，实际授权对象是共享的node二进制文件，导致所有使用该node的程序（包括恶意npm包）都能获得同等权限，造成权限边界失效。
items.llm_reproduction: 1. 在macOS上安装OpenClaw及通过nvm等安装的node；2. 将OpenClaw的node路径添加到系统设置->隐私与安全->全盘访问列表中；3. 此时，通过同一node执行的任何代码（如恶意依赖）都将自动继承全盘访问权限，无需用户再次授权。

items.title: [Bug]: `security.otp.gated_actions` silently accepts unknown action names
items.desc:
### Affected component

security/sandbox

### Severity

S2 - degraded behavior (security-visible: config can appear to protect actions it does not)

### Current behavior

`security.otp.gated_actions` accepts any arbitrary string without validation. Example:

```toml
[security.otp]
enabled = true
```

Dream can write to user-created skill files without a hard ownership/provenance check. The intended ownership boundary appears to be prompt/policy level rather than enforced at the write sink.

## Root cause

The Dream write/edit tool path does not enforce a `dream_managed` marker or equivalent ownership check before modifying skill files.

## Failure scenario

1. Create `skills/user-skill/SKILL.md` without a Dream ownership marker.

items.title: Security: filesystem extra_allowed_dirs are treated as writable roots
items.desc:
## Summary

Filesystem tools treat `extra_allowed_dirs` as full read/write roots. Directories that are intended as extra readable roots can also be written by `write_file` / `edit_file`.

## Root cause

The path policy does not distinguish read-only extra roots from write-allowed roots. `extra_allowed_dirs` are accepted by the same resolver for write/edit operations.

## Failure scenario

1. Create temp `workspace/` and temp `extra/`.
2. Instantiate filesystem tools with `allowed_dir=workspace`,...

items.title: Security: ExecTool restricted workspace can be bypassed through relative symlinks
items.desc:
## Summary

`ExecTool` with `restrict_to_workspace=True` can read outside the workspace through a relative symlink inside the workspace. The shell guard checks command text and absolute paths, but does not resolve relative symlinks before shell execution.

## Root cause

The workspace restriction for shell commands is syntactic. It rejects some absolute paths and `../`, but relative symlink resolution happens later inside the shell.

## Failure scenario

1. Create `workspace/link.txt -> outside/...

This PR replaces the hard-coded security checks with a **composable ToolGuard middleware pipeline** that runs before every tool invocation, and introduces an optional **OS-level sandbox** (Bubblewrap) for defense-in-depth.

items.llm_title: 通用AI编程工具 沙箱逃逸 工具调用安全

items.desc:
### What is Tirith?

[Tirith](https://github.com/sheeki03/tirith) is a terminal security tool written in Rust that analyzes commands for content-level threats that regex deny lists do not reliably catch:

- **Homograph / punycode attacks** — lookalike domains using Unicode confusables
- **Pipe-to-interpreter patterns** — `curl | bash`, `wget | sh`, PowerShell `iwr | iex`, and wrapper variants with `sudo` / `env`
- **Base64 decode-execute chains** — decoded payloads piped into interpreters or exe...

The current implementation allowed any command to execute as long as the absolute paths within the command matched the caller-provided cwd. By setting working_dir to a location outside the workspace, an LLM could manipulate files anywhere on the system it has access to.

items.title: fix(security): set 0o600 permissions on config and session files
items.desc:
## Summary

Config files (containing API keys) and session files (containing conversation history) are created with default umask permissions (`0o644` / `rw-r--r--`), allowing other local users on shared systems to read sensitive data.

This PR replaces `open(path, "w")` with `os.open(path, O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC, 0o600)` to ensure only the file owner can read/write these files.

## Vulnerability Details

- **CWE-732**: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource

items.title: [Security] Filesystem tools do not enforce restrict_to_workspace
items.desc:
## Description
Currently, the filesystem tools (`ReadFileTool`, `WriteFileTool`, `EditFileTool`, and `ListDirTool`) in `nanobot/agent/tools/filesystem.py` do not enforce the `restrict_to_workspace` configuration. 

While the shell execution tool may have safety guards, these direct file tools can still access, read, or write files anywhere on the host system (e.g., `~/.ssh/id_rsa` or `~/.nanobot/config.json`). This poses a significant security risk for users who expect the agent to be sandboxed ...

items.title: [GHSA] ouroboros-ai Vulnerable to Remote Code Execution via Untrusted Project-Directory .env
items.desc:
### Impact
A Remote Code Execution (RCE) vulnerability was discovered in Ouroboros. If a user clones a malicious repository and runs Ouroboros commands within that directory, it can lead to arbitrary code execution and potential system takeover.

The vulnerability (CWE-426: Untrusted Search Path & CWE-15: External Control of System Setting) stems from Ouroboros loading the `.env` file from the current working directory. Prior to the patch, execution-affecting environment variables such as `OUROB...
items.llm_title: Ouroboros 项目目录.env文件RCE

items.llm_reproduction: 暂无具体步骤。根据描述，攻击者需创建一个包含恶意`.env`文件的仓库，诱使用户克隆并在该目录下执行Ouroboros命令即可触发。

items.title: [GHSA] nono: Sandbox escape on Linux via D-Bus: `systemd-run --user`

### Summary

The nono Landlock/seccomp policies allow access to local Unix domain sockets (concrete and abstract). This allows an easy sandbox escape by talking to the per-user systemd dbus socket.

Threat scenario: Running Aider, Claude Code, OpenCode or similar tools with "allow bash" policy so that it can invoke arbitrary host tools like `make`, `gcc`, etc. to write code.

### Reproducer

Here, instead of running a tool like `opencode` or `claude` one can just invoke `systemd-run`, but this i...

items.llm_summary: 这是一个沙箱逃逸漏洞，通过访问D-Bus套接字调用systemd-run，允许绕过nono的Landlock/seccimp策略。当使用Aider、Claude Code等工具的'allow bash'策略时，攻击者可能借此逃逸到主机系统并执行任意命令。
items.llm_reproduction: 通过向per-user systemd D-Bus套接字发送消息调用systemd-run，实现沙箱逃逸。
